etymology.interpretation: The character 慶 (qìng, 'celebrate') originally combines 廌 (zhì, 'antelope') and 心 (xīn, 'heart'). The antelope, due to its beauty, symbolizes something worth celebrating or a blessing when obtained, while the heart represents emotions like goodness. This combination conveys the idea of celebration through the joy and positivity associated with possessing something precious.
etymology.simplification: The traditional character 慶 was simplified to 庆 by eliminating the bottom right component and replacing it with 大 (dà, 'big').

examples.chinese: 我们将在周末庆祝你的生日(我們將在週末慶祝你的生日)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen jiāng zài zhōumò qìngzhù nǐ de shēngrì.
examples.english: We will celebrate your birthday on the weekend.

examples.chinese: 每年国庆节我们都会放假(每年國慶節我們都會放假)。
examples.pinyin: Měinián guóqìng jié wǒmen dōu huì fàngjià.
examples.english: We have a holiday every National Day.

examples.chinese: 他们为新年的到来举办了盛大的庆典(他們為新年的到來舉辦了盛大的慶典)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen wèi xīnnián de dàolái jǔbànle shèngdà de qìngdiǎn.
examples.english: They held a grand celebration for the arrival of the New Year.
